findings.title: Reviewer can clear the active parent lease before minting writeback authority
findings.rationale: The repair depends on validate_parent_loop_authority_for_mission_mutation rejecting parent-only commands while an active loop lease exists, but clear_ralph_loop_lease removes that lease without requiring the parent loop authority token or even a mission-bound input. After a child reviewer calls clear-loop-lease, the guard falls through the no-lease branch, so capture-review-truth-snapshot can mint a fresh writeback token and record-review-outcome or compile-review-bundle can proceed without the parent-held CODEX1_PARENT_LOOP_AUTHORITY_TOKEN. This leaves the stated reviewer-lane contract unenforced for an active parent review loop because the reviewer can first delete the capability boundary.
findings.suggested_next_action: Protect clear-loop-lease, and likely pause-loop-lease/begin replacement paths, with the same active parent authority verifier when a verifier-backed active lease exists; add a regression that an untrusted reviewer lane cannot clear/pause/replace the lease and then call capture-review-truth-snapshot, compile-review-bundle, or record-review-outcome.
